title: What's a Ten-Bagger?
content:
People always ask me about 'ten-baggers.' It's a baseball term—a batter who gets to third base is a three-bagger. But in stocks? A ten-bagger is a stock that goes up 10x.

Now here's the thing—you don't need to find many. In my 13 years at Magellan, I owned about 1,400 stocks. Most of them did okay. Some lost money. But a handful? They were ten-baggers. Some were twenty-baggers. One was a FORTY-bagger.

Those few big winners made the whole fund. They paid for all my mistakes and then some.

That's why I say—you only need a few good ideas. You don't need to be right every time. You just need to let your winners run.
